publishedAbstract: Apache Kafka is a high-throughput, distributed messaging system that relies heavily on disk performance for log storage. When deployed on OpenShift, ensuring optimal disk and IOPS configurations is critical to maintaining performance, reliability, and scalability. This article provides recommendations for selecting the best storage types, configuring persistent volumes, and calculating appropriate IOPS values for Kafka brokers running in an OpenShift environment. These best practices help prevent performance degradation, reduce latency, and improve overall system efficiency.
publishedTitle: Recommended Disk and IOPS Settings for Apache Kafka Brokers on OpenShift
